dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses volume surge to confirm breakout validity. Entry: (1) Price breaks above recent swing high (approximated as Donchian upper), (2) Volume ratio vs 20-day average >= 1.5x (50% increase), (3) Close remains above breakout level. Exit: Close below breakout level (approximated as prior close since exact swing high not available as historical reference). The '33% retracement' exit condition cannot be precisely translated without invention of new indicators, so primary exit uses close reversal. The exclusion of marginal volume increases (below 10%) is implicitly handled by the 1.5x multiplier threshold. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal can apply to both long and short breakouts depending on market context.

source_rule_text: Use volume surge as a filter to confirm breakout validity before entry, avoiding whipsaw trades on breakouts lacking conviction.
